content.question: If cohort decline accelerates as projected (5% by 2033), at what pupil enrollment levels do rural single-class-per-grade schools become fiscally or operationally unviable, and what are the feasible consolidation or specialist-service-delivery alternatives?
content.reduction_path: Model forward local education budgets under regional cohort-decline scenarios; identify break-even enrollment levels for small schools by cost structure; conduct scenario analysis of consolidation pathways (e.g., multi-site specialist centres, itinerant specialist deployment, distance learning support) with cost-effectiveness estimates for serving dispersed small-school populations including SEN-identified students
